title: The 5 Wood

jokes.text: Don't let that pas de bourrée stuff... Oh no, I'm not worried about it. I mean, I'll get it.
jokes.setup: Larry is taking dance lessons and struggling with ballet terminology
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of the complex French dance term he can barely pronounce
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's overconfidence about something he clearly struggles with

jokes.text: Larry immediately wanting to pitch mixed nut ideas to a stranger's father after learning about Health-Glo
jokes.setup: Schwimmer mentions his dad owns Health-Glo mixed nuts
jokes.punchline: Larry instantly sees business opportunity and wants to pitch ideas to someone he's never met
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry behavior - turning casual conversation into personal opportunity. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate mixed nut combo pitch: 'pecan and a blueberry...dried cherry...a filbert'
jokes.setup: Larry wants to pitch nut ideas
jokes.punchline: His 'brilliant' idea is just random ingredients with no rationale
jokes.explanation: Larry's confidence in mundane observations played for comedy

jokes.text: If I said I want to combine a pecan and a blueberry, you know? And a dried cherry, and maybe a, uh, a filbert or something?
jokes.setup: Larry's initial offer to pitch nut ideas
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific and questionable combination suggestions, ending with the obscure 'filbert'
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the nut obsession with hilariously specific but questionable combinations

jokes.text: Schwimmer's dismissive 'stay in your element' to Larry's nut pitches
jokes.setup: Larry keeps pushing his nut business ideas
jokes.punchline: Polite but firm rejection of Larry's enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Larry's persistence

jokes.text: Larry repeatedly saying 'fierce' and adopting dance terminology
jokes.setup: Larry has been in dance rehearsals with gay choreographer Steve
jokes.punchline: He's unconsciously adopted gay mannerisms and vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag that will pay off later. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: You're embarrassing me. Stop it, no. - I can't have Pirate's Booty? - No, but you can have a pear.
jokes.setup: Larry apparently singing about Pirate's Booty in public
jokes.punchline: Cheryl treating him like a child and offering a pear as substitute
jokes.explanation: Funny marital dynamic and the absurdity of adult Larry being told he can have a pear instead

jokes.text: Larry examining Health-Glo packages and counting cashews: 'There's only four cashews in here'
jokes.setup: Larry spots Schwimmer's dad's product in store
jokes.punchline: He obsessively counts nuts to validate his complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsession with minor injustices. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: 'Schwimmer's dad's pulling a fast one'
jokes.punchline: He immediately assumes malicious intent from someone he's never met
jokes.explanation: Escalation from disappointment to conspiracy theory is very Larry

jokes.text: Dental hygienist asking Larry out because he 'reminded her of her college history teacher'
jokes.punchline: Her reason is he reminds her of her boring old teacher
jokes.explanation: Great setup/punchline structure with efficient character revelation

jokes.text: I was just really trying to get some cotton out of my mouth.
jokes.setup: Dental hygienist thinking Larry gave her a 'tongue sign'
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent explanation for what she interpreted as flirtation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - the misunderstanding is believable and embarrassing

jokes.text: 'Was he an avuncular bald Jew?'
jokes.setup: Hygienist says he reminds her of history teacher
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutally accurate self-description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-awareness delivered with clinical precision. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Jeff encouraging Larry to sleep with the hygienist: 'It's a gift from your wife'
jokes.punchline: Jeff frames adultery as anniversary present logic
jokes.explanation: Dark rationalization delivered with conviction. Very quotable premise.

jokes.text: Jeff calling out Larry's gay mannerisms: 'you're talking really gay'
jokes.punchline: Jeff directly confronts the behavior change
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the running gag. Direct confrontation creates comedy.

jokes.text: 'You've become Steve the gay choreographer'
jokes.punchline: Complete personality absorption diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Escalates the observation to full identity crisis. Economical and clear.

jokes.text: 'I thought he had the good Hodgkin's'
jokes.setup: Learning someone died of Hodgkin's disease
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate casual distinction about cancer types
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Larry's signature inappropriate casualness

jokes.text: Larry defending his Hodgkin's knowledge with 'Party of Five' as medical authority
jokes.punchline: Cites TV drama as medical expertise
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - using teen drama as medical reference. Classic Larry logic.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, I thought Sven was a Swedish name. - It's not. 'Cause I don't look Swedish, do I?
jokes.punchline: Sven's indignant response about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Decent setup about cultural assumptions and touchy nationalism

jokes.text: Sven's passionate defense: 'Culture, looks, names, history, food, dancing'
jokes.setup: Larry asks what's the difference between Swedes and Norwegians
jokes.punchline: Sven rattles off an exhaustive list like it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how seriously Sven takes the distinction

jokes.text: Larry saving the Health-Glo package to show Schwimmer the cashew problem
jokes.punchline: He's been carrying evidence around like a lawyer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry obsessiveness - treating snack food like a legal case

jokes.text: Schwimmer's explanation about cashew shortage in the industry
jokes.punchline: Elaborate industry explanation for minor snack ratios
jokes.explanation: Straight-man response that adds absurd gravity to trivial complaint

jokes.text: Larry's rebranding suggestion: 'I would call it raisins'
jokes.punchline: Larry's solution is to lower expectations rather than improve product
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic - solve disappointment through lowered expectations

jokes.text: Larry getting a letter from the club for having a dirty locker
jokes.punchline: It's about locker cleanliness like he's a child
jokes.explanation: Sets up the club consequences storyline with appropriate absurdity

jokes.text: Norm's story about Burt Suzuki being kicked out for filthy locker
jokes.punchline: Precedent of actual member expulsion for hygiene
jokes.explanation: Escalates the stakes while maintaining the absurd premise

jokes.text: Larry asking Marty for the weatherman's tip at his father's funeral
jokes.punchline: Larry pivots to personal golf advice request
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry inappropriate timing. Perfect character consistency in worst possible context.

jokes.text: Larry finding and interrogating the 'Party of Five' writer at the funeral
jokes.setup: Jeff mentions the writer is at the funeral
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately hunts him down for medical verification
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback escalation. Larry's obsession with being right trumps funeral decorum.

jokes.text: The good vs. better Hodgkin's semantic debate
jokes.setup: Writer explains Hodgkin's vs non-Hodgkin's survival rates
jokes.punchline: Larry insists 'better' equals 'good' in an endless semantic loop
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's inability to concede even when corrected by experts

jokes.text: The entire good Hodgkin's vs better Hodgkin's semantic argument
jokes.punchline: Larry insists on 'good' vs 'better' semantics at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Sustained cringe comedy. Larry's need to win semantic arguments regardless of context.

jokes.text: Larry discovering his 5-wood is in the casket
jokes.setup: Looking at open casket at funeral
jokes.punchline: Larry's golf club has been buried with the deceased
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected escalation. Sets up the episode's central absurd conflict.

jokes.text: 'Sven must have put my club in there, probably on purpose'
jokes.setup: Finding his golf club in casket
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately assumes malicious intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect paranoid Larry logic connecting earlier conflict to current problem

jokes.text: 'That club's irreplaceable. It's 10 years old. They don't make it.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why he needs his club back
jokes.punchline: His attachment to this specific club reaches absurd proportions
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Larry's material obsessions trumping social norms

jokes.text: Larry arguing why the dead man shouldn't be 'buried in eternity with my club'
jokes.punchline: Larry frames it as unfair to him rather than respecting the dead
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold. Larry's self-centeredness reaches absurd heights.

jokes.text: 'Why should this guy be buried in eternity with my club? That's not fair.'
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting Larry leave the club with the dead man
jokes.punchline: Larry frames it as a cosmic injustice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Larry's selfishness expressed as moral outrage

jokes.text: Larry taking a cashew from the casket during the eulogy
jokes.setup: Eulogy in progress, Larry approaches casket
jokes.punchline: He steals a snack from the dead man during the service
jokes.explanation: Incredible visual gag combining multiple storylines in most inappropriate way possible

jokes.text: Visual gag of Larry discretely reaching into the casket during the eulogy
jokes.punchline: Larry attempting to retrieve his golf club while eulogy continues
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe comedy - visual desecration during sacred moment, perfectly staged

jokes.text: Jeff's expletive-filled rant about being kicked out over 'a fucking 5-iron'
jokes.punchline: Jeff's disproportionate rage at the consequences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing consequences of Larry's actions on others

jokes.text: 'You're fucking narcissists...It's all a game, all a big fucking game'
jokes.punchline: Accurate diagnosis delivered with fury
jokes.explanation: Jeff becomes voice of audience frustration with Larry's selfishness

jokes.text: 'there's like three fucking Jews in the whole club'
jokes.punchline: Frank assessment of club demographics
jokes.explanation: Direct commentary on country club exclusivity

jokes.text: 'I'm more of a Jew-face than you?'
jokes.setup: Jeff says Larry would stick out
jokes.punchline: Larry takes offense at not being Jewish enough
jokes.explanation: Absurd competition over Jewish appearance. Very quotable Larry logic.

jokes.text: 'The dog bit my penis!'
jokes.punchline: Shocking revelation about what happened
jokes.explanation: Shocking reveal with perfect economy. Instantly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: 'Why did the dog give you an erection?'
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's horrifying interpretation
jokes.explanation: Escalates to worst possible interpretation. Peak cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Ted Danson connection: 'Is that why Ted Danson didn't want you to take care of his dog?'
jokes.setup: Pattern of Larry having dog problems
jokes.punchline: Revelation that this connects to previous celebrity incidents
jokes.explanation: Good escalation connecting current incident to Larry's history

jokes.text: Doctor Anthony turning out to be Steve's friend from dance rehearsals
jokes.punchline: Connects back to dance storyline coincidentally
jokes.explanation: Nice structural callback that connects disparate storylines

jokes.text: 'I would call it May-October. I wouldn't say December'
jokes.punchline: Larry quibbles over age gap terminology
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry precision about meaningless distinctions

jokes.text: Larry's fake WASP persona: 'May-October relationship' correction
jokes.setup: Playing wealthy Republican for country club interview
jokes.punchline: Larry can't help but make pedantic corrections even while lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry - his compulsive honesty sabotages his own deception

jokes.text: Larry's elaborate polo backstory with mallet head injury
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated aristocratic sports injury
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of fake backstory with specific details

jokes.text: Larry producing doctor's note from Dr. Anthony Parker for golf cart
jokes.setup: Needing medical excuse for cart use
jokes.punchline: Uses the penis-bite doctor for fake medical authorization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that ties multiple storylines together perfectly. Great structural comedy.

jokes.text: Larry canceling the dental hygienist date because 'a dog bit my penis'
jokes.punchline: Most absurd excuse delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback closing. Absurd excuse delivered with Larry's signature directness.

tmdb_overview: A bag of cashews and rasins threatens Larry's working relationship with David Schwimmer; a dirty locker and a golf club threatens his club membership; Larry gets into a compromising situation with a dog.
